name: penumbra-rhino-01-gyroid
description: [DEPRECATED — out-of-process image-blit fallback] Gate ▣1 — a real CPig gyroid (clipped to a 150-unit sphere) rendered live in the Penumbra viewport. Loads 53_penumbra_preview.json via the Slop loader (Field TPMS gyroid ∩ sphere → Penumbra Preview), runs PenumbraOopShow (formerly PenumbraShow), waits for a REAL (non-companion) frame, captures the renderer's dumped PNG via file-source. This is the one Penumbra rhino test still exercising the CPig→Penumbra live-preview chain (the OOP path); it survives until the in-process GLSL studio's CPig integration lands. Requires the Penumbra Rhino plug-in registered for auto-load + the CPig .gha (with Penumbra Preview, GUID …BA200) loaded. NOTE: until the conduit's view auto-fit lands, this relies on the 150-unit clip sphere framing in Rhino's default empty-doc camera (~315 units out); a smaller field would be sub-pixel (see hosts/rhino/LESSONS-rhino-viewer-integration.md §5).

setup.vlmDescription: A Penumbra SDF render of a gyroid lattice (smooth, repeating, interconnected curved surfaces) filling a roughly spherical region, drawn as an overlay in the Rhino viewport. NOT a plain featureless sphere, NOT an empty grey viewport.
